name: astrodynamics
description: Orbital mechanics simulation engine. Integrates spacecraft trajectories using a Symplectic Euler integrator with a cached Float64Array state array. All functions share a single cache keyed by slider state — the cache is rebuilt only when slider values change. Functions are available in the expression sandbox after importing this domain.

implementation.integrator: Symplectic Euler (semi-implicit Euler). At each step: velocity is updated first using acceleration at current position, then position is updated using the new velocity. This preserves energy better than standard (explicit) Euler for conservative systems like gravity.
implementation.stepCount: n = clamp(round(T / 1.25), 800, 6000). Time step dt = T / n.
implementation.storage: Trajectory stored as Float64Array typed arrays (arrT, arrX, arrY, arrVx, arrVy, arrR, arrVr, arrVt, arrHit). Lookup is O(1) index calculation + linear interpolation between two adjacent steps.
implementation.cache: Cache key is a string of all slider values. If any slider changes, the entire trajectory is re-integrated from t=0. All 7 public functions share the same cache.
implementation.gravitationalParameter: mu = Gs * Mx * 10000  (km³/s²). With Earth defaults: mu ≈ 398600 km³/s².
implementation.coordinateSystem: 2D simulation plane. Planet center at origin. Launch point at (Rp+h, 0). Positive y is 'up' from launch. Radial unit vector: er = (x,y)/r. Tangential unit vector: et = (-y,x)/r (prograde direction).

equations.gravity: a_gravity = -mu / r^3 * (x, y)  — inverse square law in vector form

implementation.impactDetection: After each Euler step, if hypot(x,y) <= Rp the spacecraft is considered to have hit the surface. Position is snapped to the surface (scaled to Rp), velocity zeroed, and hit=true for all subsequent steps.

note: All functions read slider values by ID at call time via _sliderValueNum(). The scene must define sliders with the IDs listed in sliderContracts for the simulation to respond to user input.

phi.description: Launch angle from vertical (deg). 0 = straight up, 90 = horizontal prograde.

vlaunch.description: Initial speed for coast mode (km/s). Applied at angle phi from vertical.

athrust.description: Thrust acceleration powered mode (km/s²). Applied at angle phi for tburn seconds.

tburn.description: Burn duration powered mode (s). Thrust cuts off after this time.

athrust1.description: First-stage thrust acceleration (km/s²). Applied during [0, tburn1] with pitch-over.

athrust2.description: Second-stage thrust acceleration (km/s²). Applied during [tburn1+tcoast, tburn1+tcoast+tburn2] targeting circular orbit.

tburn2.description: Second burn duration (s). Thrust direction is actively steered to match target circular orbit speed at h_target.

pitch_start.description: Initial pitch angle (deg) at t=0 in guided mode. 90 = vertical, 0 = horizontal.

pitch_end.description: Final pitch angle (deg) at end of pitch-over maneuver.

tpitch.description: Duration (s) of the pitch-over gravity turn. Pitch interpolates linearly from pitch_start to pitch_end over [0, tpitch].

modes.name: coast
modes.description: Ballistic trajectory from initial velocity, no thrust.
modes.implementation: Initial velocity: vx = vlaunch * sin(phi), vy = vlaunch * cos(phi). Only gravity acts throughout. Closed-form Keplerian orbit for constant mu, but integrated numerically for consistency with other modes.

modes.name: powered
modes.description: Constant thrust at fixed angle for tburn seconds.
modes.implementation: Initial velocity: vx = v0 * sin(phi), vy = v0 * cos(phi). For t <= tburn, acceleration = a_gravity + athrust * (sin(phi)*er + cos(phi)*et). After tburn, coast only. Thrust direction is fixed to launch angle phi — it does not steer.

modes.name: guided
modes.description: Two-burn ascent with pitch-over gravity turn and active orbital insertion targeting.
modes.implementation: Phase 1 [0, tburn1]: thrust athrust1 with pitch angle interpolating from pitch_start to pitch_end over tpitch seconds. Pitch bias = phi - 90 deg added to align with launch site. Phase 2 [tburn1, tburn1+tcoast]: coast, no thrust. Phase 3 [tburn1+tcoast, tburn1+tcoast+tburn2]: active guidance — thrust athrust2 split between tangential (88%) and radial (34%) components, steered to close the error between current vt and v_circ(h_target), and drive vr toward zero.

orbitX.description: Spacecraft x-coordinate (km) in the simulation plane at time t.
orbitX.implementation: Linearly interpolates between precomputed arrX[i0] and arrX[i1] where i0 = floor(t/dt). Planet center is at (0,0). Launch starts at x = Rp+h, y = 0.

orbitY.description: Spacecraft y-coordinate (km) in the simulation plane at time t.
orbitY.implementation: Linearly interpolates arrY. See orbitX for coordinate system details.

orbitR.description: Spacecraft distance from planet center (km) at time t.
orbitR.implementation: r = hypot(x, y) recomputed from interpolated (x, y). Equals Rp at surface, Rp+h at launch.
orbitR.returns: number — radial distance in km. Altitude above surface = orbitR(t,mode) - Rp.

orbitVr.description: Radial velocity component (km/s) — rate of change of distance from planet center.
orbitVr.implementation: vr = vx * (x/r) + vy * (y/r) = v · er. Positive = moving away from planet (ascending). Negative = approaching (descending). For a stable circular orbit, vr = 0.

orbitVt.description: Tangential (orbital) velocity component (km/s) — the sideways speed perpendicular to the radial direction.
orbitVt.implementation: vt = -vx * (y/r) + vy * (x/r) = v · et. This is the component that sustains orbit. Circular orbit condition: vt = sqrt(mu/r), vr = 0. For Earth LEO at r = 6771 km: vt ≈ 7.67 km/s.
orbitVt.returns: number — tangential speed in km/s. Positive = prograde (counterclockwise). Negative = retrograde.

orbitHit.description: Returns 1 if the spacecraft has impacted the planet surface at or before time t, 0 otherwise.
orbitHit.implementation: Impact is detected when hypot(x,y) <= Rp after an integration step. Once hit, arrHit[i]=1 for all i >= impact index. orbitHit returns 1 if arrHit[i0] or arrHit[i1] is set, or if t >= hitTime.
orbitHit.returns: number — 1 (impact) or 0 (no impact). Useful for conditional color: orbitHit(t,1) ? '#ff0000' : '#00ff00'.

orbitOutcome.description: Returns a human-readable outcome string classifying the trajectory status at time t.
orbitOutcome.implementation: Evaluated at the given t: (1) if hit or r <= Rp+1e-3 → impact. (2) if v >= 0.995 * v_esc → escape. (3) if burn is complete and |r - r_target| <= 40 km and |vt - v_circ(r_target)| <= 0.25 km/s and |vr| <= 0.08 km/s → stable orbit achieved. Otherwise → bound but not stabilized. r_target = Rp + h_target. v_circ = sqrt(mu / r_target). v_esc = sqrt(2 * mu / r).

values: Outcome: bound but not yet stabilized
